rationale: In the citing paper, Fang et al. (2011) is cited directly in the Measurement section as the source for how industry competitiveness is calculated. This is a concrete methodological use tied to one of the model variables, so the citation has a functional role beyond a generic literature-list mention. However, the citing paper does not engage the cited paper's core customer-innovation asset configuration arguments or findings in its theory development. The role is therefore best characterized as applying a prior operationalization.
relation_description: The citing paper adopts Fang et al. (2011) as a precedent for operationalizing industry competitiveness (1 - Herfindahl index) in its empirical models. It uses that measurement approach in a new context (plural vs. dyadic marketing alliances) rather than building on the cited paper's asset-configuration theory.
